draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer persistent navigation on the left with glanceable stats stacked vertically, rather than tab switching that breaks flow
facade.label: Sidebar with weather cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values keeping nav always visible and wants vertical card stacking for quick scans mid-run
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers tab switching or horizontal layouts; may want full-screen focus on single data view

facade.hypothesis: Runners respond better to encouraging, celebratory language than neutral, factual tone
facade.label: Motivational cheers
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use upbeat, encouraging language—'Nice pace!' 'You crushed that mile!'—creating an energetic, celebratory personality that cheers runners on.
facade.rejectImplies: The app should adopt a neutral, straightforward tone focused on data and facts—'Current pace: 8:30/mi'—prioritizing clarity and professionalism over emotional engagement.

evidence.hypothesis: Runners prefer persistent navigation on the left with glanceable stats stacked vertically, rather than tab switching that breaks flow
evidence.implication: User values keeping nav always visible and wants vertical card stacking for quick scans mid-run

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer high-contrast, distraction-free interfaces that prioritize readability in sunlight
facade.axisTargeted: Visual clarity and light vs dark
facade.label: Bright and minimal
facade.acceptImplies: User values clean typography, ample whitespace, and high luminosity for outdoor visibility—likely wants data-forward design over decorative elements
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer darker, more atmospheric interfaces or denser information layouts; visual drama matters more than pure functional clarity

facade.hypothesis: Runners want location context (map) AND numerical data (pace/distance) simultaneously, not sequentially
facade.label: Split view: map and stats
facade.acceptImplies: Design should prioritize dual-panel layouts that show real-time location + metrics side-by-side; avoid full-screen map with hidden stats
facade.rejectImplies: Design should integrate data INTO map (overlays, badges) or prioritize one over the other; sequential views acceptable

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer warm, energetic visuals that evoke outdoor conditions rather than clinical interface design
facade.label: Glowing gradient sunset
facade.acceptImplies: Runner audience responds to atmospheric, warmth-forward design; weather app benefits from mood-setting over pure data clarity
facade.rejectImplies: Runners prioritize function and legibility; gradients and glow effects read as distraction; prefer stark, high-contrast, data-first aesthetic

facade.hypothesis: Runners need visual feedback that feels effortless, not jarring—transitions that match the flow of running itself rather than interrupting it.
facade.label: Smooth fade in
facade.acceptImplies: User values continuity and organic visual rhythm; likely prefers animation over instant state changes; may want app to feel meditative or immersive.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clarity and speed over motion; likely wants snappy, instant UI responses; may find animations distracting during activity.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance widgets on home screen vs. navigating into detailed forecast views
Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. neutral, factual weather reporting
Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented vibe for pre-run prep
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather data
Whether user prefers smooth, flowing transitions vs. snappy, instant feedback when checking conditions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance widgets on home screen vs. navigating into detailed forecast views
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. neutral, factual weather reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented vibe for pre-run prep
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, flowing transitions vs. snappy, instant feedback when checking conditions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runners in motion prioritize scanning speed and minimal visual breaks over spacious card layouts; tight line-by-line weather data (temp, wind, humidity) in a scrollable list loads faster cognitively than separated card containers
facade.label: Dense list, no cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values information density and rapid eye-scanning over visual breathing room; likely prefers utilitarian interfaces during active running
facade.rejectImplies: User needs visual separation and spaciousness to process information safely while moving; card-based layouts with breathing room feel more natural than compressed lists

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind, humidity, and pace-friendly forecasts to help runners plan their routes and timing.
